value_chain_position: Analog and embedded processor semiconductors — sells power management, signal conditioning and MCU silicon into automotive, industrial, data-center power and communications end-markets.
relative_comps: TXN is the largest pure-play analog semiconductor company; its industrial and automotive exposure (~65% of revenue combined) gives it energy-transition linkage via EV/grid applications, but it is not a high-growth AI play — 3-year revenue CAGR is negative (-4%). The company is in a heavy capex investment cycle (300mm fab buildout) that is compressing near-term FCF. Analog Devices is the closest peer; both trade at similar 25-28x forward earnings multiples. TXN's competitive moat is broad product breadth and customer stickiness, not a chokepoint position.
